name: docker-mcp-server
description: Manage Docker through the Docker SDK for Python and the docker CLI: containers, images, networks, volumes, Compose, Swarm, Buildx, Scout, and OCI registries, exposed as MCP tools.
long_description:
Manage one or more Docker daemons through your AI agent — containers, images, networks, volumes, Compose, Swarm, Buildx, Scout, and OCI registries, exposed as MCP tools.

Discovery is automatic for your local socket with no configuration required. Or point it at additional remote daemons over TCP, TLS, or SSH (and mark any of them read-only to monitor without the risk of accidental changes). It also surfaces logs and stats as resources for triage. Read-only / no-destructive / disabled-domain switches let you register a restricted tool surface.

Answer questions like: ‘why did my local container crash?’, ‘is my production system under memory pressure?’, ‘what differences are there between my staging and production systems?’

Runs entirely on your machine and sends no telemetry — see the Privacy Policy.

docker_hosts.title: Docker host(s)
docker_hosts.description: Docker daemon(s) to manage. Blank = your default Docker context / socket. One remote: ssh://user@host (recommended; auth via your SSH keys), tcp://host:2375 for a plaintext TCP daemon, or tcp://host:2376(tls=/path/to/certs) for a TLS daemon (the directory needs ca.pem, which verifies the daemon — a self-signed daemon works — plus cert.pem/key.pem only if it requires a client cert). Several: comma-separated name=endpoint pairs, e.g. local=auto, prod=ssh://ops@prod.example.com(ro) — append (ro) to make a host read-only. When set, this replaces DOCKER_HOST.

read_only.title: Read-only mode
read_only.description: Register only read-only tools — no create, modify, or delete.

no_destructive.title: Disable destructive tools
no_destructive.description: Register everything except destructive tools (remove / prune / kill).

disable_domains.title: Disabled domains
disable_domains.description: Comma-separated tool domains to drop, e.g. swarm,plugins,scout.
